text: He's right. I'll use Wirtz as an example and just to say I don't like Liverpool so this isn't a fan view. Wirtz has had a lot of stick this season because his goals and assists stats are low, people are obsessed with stats and measure performance on it, however I watch a lot of football, any team I can if its on TV and like to look at what players are doing and Wirtz will often make the pass before the assist or in the build up and its often a pass others aren't capable of making but without it they couldn't break through a team, he gets no assist or credit for the goal and people say he's bad but those people are totally missing his contribution to the goal. Carrick was often underrated at United for the same reasons as another example. Fans don't pay enough attention to the game, they see the goals and assists and base performance off that and not what players are actually doing. I often think too many fans aren't actual football fans because they are blinded by bias to their club, to their players, hatred against the rest and see stats as the only metric to judge players on, they aren't real football fans that actually watch and understand football.

text: I’m Dutch. From time to time Argentinians still complain that LVG said in 2022 that Messi was washed, past it and that we would win because Messi was bad. What Van Gaal actually said? A Dutch reporter said that the Dutch would have no chance against Argentina. So LVG responded that everything was possible. Then the reporter said Messi didn’t defend well. LVG answered that Messi was the best player ever and probably still the best player right then (2022). Journalist went on that sometimes he doesn’t defend back, would that be an advantage to the Dutch? LVG basically answered that if Messi doesn’t defend when they’re out of possession, then the Dutch are playing against 10 man and that’s an advantage. Of course the Argentine press (and to be fair also media from all other countries including NL itself) cut out the part of the journo riling LVG up by saying they had no chance, cut off the part where the journo keeps saying Messi doesn’t track back and only keep the part where Van Gaal says “Messi doesn’t defend, we’re playing against 10 man”. Van Gaal quite literally said just before that that Messi is the GOAT. Of course this was all a big thing and used by the Argentines to get Messi in the right mood. LVG should have been smarter, he knows how the world works. But it tells you all you have to know how this interview will be “translated” in Argentina.
label: analysis
